seeds.text: The car never stops peeling: one unbroken ribbon of upholstery, street, skyline — and Tina rides the still-attached end like a kite string anchored to the world she left
Raul peeling the mango reads to the crowd like pulling a fresh screen protector off reality; the bleachers whoop like the internet at a perfect peel and the scoreboard replays it in slow motion
Meg bites hers whole and every solid in the stadium gets a hovering chalk outline an inch off its surface, like the world is a sidewalk game about to start
Behind Liz and John their footsteps leave thin bright tube-lines; an unseen craftsman bends the two lines toward each other and fuses them into one glowing cursive loop, buzzing like a sign coming on
One full second of total black silence inside the mouth — then clouds part below like curtains in a very large quiet room; the candy islands do not boom, they simply are there, humming
seeds.stimulus: In total darkness, or in a very large room, very quietly

seeds.text: Not just the car: every enclosure Tina is inside peels one layer at a time — the car, then the traffic jam, then the afternoon itself; whole-biters skip layers
Raul armors up plate by plate and we never cut away — the locked camera holds while the bleachers tell the story in sound: gasps, then the marching band changing key to match him
From inside her mouth we watch the campus unbuild itself backward, brick by brick into orchard, as the soundtrack runs in reverse toward perfect quiet; off-screen a janitor sighs at his unswept steps
The last enclosure is the frame itself: Tina peels the letterbox bars off the commercial and the picture widens; whole-biters get full screen instantly
The stadium splits mid-frame into two tribes: the peeler side grows layered, terraced, bonsai-detailed; the whole-biter side goes instant total pop-color; the game plays on across the seam
Tina peels with the window down; the ribbon rides the street gutter like a marble run, overtaking the Uber — pedestrians film the peel, not her; she just chews
A uniformed inspector appears whenever someone peels in public: he checks the spiral, stamps LICENSED TO PEEL; Liz shows John her older, worn certificate — instant recognition
Raul holds out a peel strip to the bleachers like a mic; one kid takes it and gets a single mango gauntlet, and they dap with the one matched arm
Permuted: the car peels Tina — the Uber sheds its Tina layer like a sticker; she stands streetside perfectly cool while a translucent Tina outline stays behind to pay the fare
The whole spot is silver-gelatin monochrome; only what has been peeled carries color, and color travels only along peel lines, converging at the finale sky
seeds.stimulus: Black and white except one color

seeds.text: Upside-down over the bleachers: when Meg eats, shadows stop belonging to their owners and drift to pile under the most bored kid; nobody notices except one shadowless bystander, hair lifting with static
The shadow pile under the bored kid deepens into a hole; he finally looks up, peels one, and his own shadow climbs back out to him first — loyalty returned by attention
Tina peels it, eats it, nothing happens for two full seconds — then we see the Uber has been parked inside her notebook doodle all along; the margin drawing is the world of the spot
John peels his gummy into a curl and looks through it like a periscope: the lecture hall as Liz doodles it, annotated and better; he lowers it; she is already looking through hers
Before eating, every kid performs a tiny invented ritual — Raul salts the bench with candy sugar and stomps twice; the impossible thing waits politely until the ritual is done

High above the neighborhood Tina and her parachute climb through faint giant after-images of herself from each second of the ascent — a stairway of ghost Tinas curling into the clouds; she waves and the echoes wave a beat late

Generalizing the giant-hand note: the sunroof opens like lips and the whole car is drawn gently up into a sky-sized bite that turns out to be the cloud layer — the world eats the car the way she eats candy
seeds.stimulus: generalize: giant hand becomes giant mouth (script p1 note)

seeds.text: Wide on the frozen stadium, every player and bird locked mid-play; the only moving thing is Raul, forty feet tall, stepping carefully over the paused game like a kid crossing a diorama he built
seeds.stimulus: The beat contains exactly one moving thing

seeds.text: Seen only in the glass of the campus doors: a stranger bites and the reflection becomes a hand-painted mural of the quad twenty times too large; Liz and John are tiny painted figures holding their breath; on the real sidewalk the discarded peel dries alone

The Uber window is suddenly a fifteen-inch door: through it Tina sees the garden-colored world at the wrong scale, unreachable — the bite resizes HER, not the door, and she steps through leaving the car human-sized behind her

Tina leaves the car the way you leave a group chat: one bite, the seatbelt unbuckles itself, the car seals silently behind her and she is already elsewhere — inside, the driver hears the soft exit chime
seeds.stimulus: c314 group-chat exit x c204 selkie skin

seeds.text: The armor assembles as a marching-band event: each brass hit irons one mango plate onto Raul like a patch onto denim — by the key change he is a declared surface the whole stadium can read
seeds.stimulus: c326 bleacher serenade x c308 iron-on patch x c115 ribbon henshin

seeds.text: Permuted: the peel hangs perfectly still; the WORLD spins around it like a centrifuge, separating the street into its layers — asphalt, map-lines, orchard soil — while Tina holds the one still thing
seeds.stimulus: permute + centrifuge (invariant: one unbroken continuous action)

seeds.text: Generalized to a pour: the bitten gummy runs upward as one unbroken syrup thread that hardens behind her like a stalactite; Tina climbs the still-growing column out through the sunroof

The peel as monitored geological event: seismograph needles jump at the bleachers, loudspeakers announce PEEL WARNING LEVEL ORANGE, the crowd dons souvenir goggles as one continuous rind lifts off the whole field in a single slow sheet

Permuted agent: the ribbon precedes the bite — already threaded through campus railing, sleeve, and fountain like something that has been here before and will not say; when John bites, the pre-laid line pulls taut in one motion and the day gathers itself around the couple
seeds.stimulus: permute + Which character has been here before and why wont they say

seeds.text: Outside the window the neighborhood runs at lawn-sprinkler half-speed — joggers, dogs, arcs of water — except one hummingbird at real speed; her bite moves HER to the hummingbird speed and the Uber crawls beneath her attention

Outside the window, a funeral procession for the afternoon itself: hired mourners in fruit-colored veils wail beautifully for 3 PM as it passes; at her bite they pivot mid-step to celebration — the afternoon is reborn as HERS

Retyped: the sky does not eat the car, it INHALES — one long breath through the open window draws the street loose things (leaves, receipts, a kite) into slow orbit around the car, and Tina exhales to steer

The window is her feed: the neighborhood scrolls past in stories, each house a post, each pedestrian a 3-second clip with floating captions; her bite stops the scroll — one real house holds still long enough to be beautiful

Overhead god shot: at her bite the block architecture loses gravity politely — houses drift up a few feet and hover, shivering like a foot waking up; the only earthbound thing is the wrapper in the gutter, holding the neighborhood down like a paperweight
A giant hand peels open the car roof like a wrapper; Tina looks up delighted as the foil-bright metal curls back
As the car drives, everything it passes peels open in its wake — hedges, houses, the bus stop — each revealing a brighter version under the skin
Out the window, a blimp-sized goldfish swims lazily between the houses at rooftop height; sprinklers arc in its shadow
Every block out the window is a different place and season: suburb, alpine pass, rice terraces, coral shallows — same street signs throughout
The neighborhood saturates flavor-colored as she chews — lychee-pink light, mango-gold shadows (blacklist 1/2 adjacent: capture, repair before advancing)
Outside the window everything unanchored floats a few feet up — bikes, dogs mid-trot, a mail cart — while the car drives on normally
The voltron built from MULTIPLE candies: mango torso, lychee gauntlets, watermelon greaves — each limb color-blocked and glossy, assembling around Raul flavor by flavor
A stadium-sized Raul leans down into frame and eats regular Raul like a gummy — who then looks out from the giant self, now driving it
The peel whips over his shoulders as a translucent mango cape; classic hero silhouette on the bleacher top rail, wind from nowhere
The default: anime aura, speed lines, lightning, hair physics — the bleachers flatten into graphic panels (capture the default so it can be banned or repaired)
Two long peel strips lock onto his back as stiff translucent wings and he rises off the bleachers into the stadium lights
WORLD: verdant mango-grove jungle in full glory, gummy-glossy fruit at lantern scale (script native)
WORLD: underwater paradise, mango-orange fish, light caustics on their faces, hair floating (script native)
WORLD: flat-shaded old-school animation — the couple rendered rubber-hose while holding real hands (script native)
WORLD: claymation campus, thumbprints visible in their clay faces, stop-motion stutter in their walk (script native)
WORLD: candy cityscape, gummy architecture, licorice lampposts (blacklist 2: capture; needs repair to advance)
WORLD: zero-g space with peeled-fruit planets, a lychee moon half-skinned, its peel in orbit like rings
WORLD: the couple walks a giant picnic table — thumb-sized under a bag of Peelerz the size of a building, napkin dunes
WORLD: neon night-city, wet asphalt, signs in fruit glyphs, their reflections in colored puddles

The roof peels in one continuous curl following her exact thumb gesture from the CU — same motion at two scales — and the canopy forms from the peeled layer itself: translucent gummy membrane, sun glowing through; in the finale she tears a strip off her own chute and eats it
The window itself peels: the fog wipes away as a film layer and the candy world was already behind the glass — transformation as disclosure rather than explosion

Seatbelt as ripcord and harness: the car peels away around her and she rides the seat up, arriving at the sky finale still sitting in an Uber seat — Bens gummy pod seat becomes her gondola, the pod cluster detaching and rising with her in it

The driver, deadpan in the rearview as his car comes apart: you want me to end the ride? Variant: bored disassembly — he calmly peels off mirror, dashboard, windshield, stacks them on the passenger seat like he does this every shift, then peels his own door and steps out onto the moving street
The asphalt peels up from the horizon like a giant pull-tab and the car rides the curling crest — the peel itself as launch ramp (kin: v02 render's lane-running ribbon)

She looks out and the horizon has a rim — suburbia is a box of assorted Peelerz, houses in paper cups, and the giant hand hovers over the tray choosing; it picks her car like a chocolate. TENSION: premise-scale, fights Bens street-level causal frame
Passing car at a stoplight: the other passenger is her, peeling a tiny car whose roof peels in sync with her own — peeler and peeled in one beat. TENSION: reads vertigo rather than release

The giant hand peels the roof and eats THE PEEL, casually, while lifting her out — because the peel is candy; gummy-corrected from the feral hand-eats-car gag into product truth (crunch, muffled five stars please as variant)

The sky develops a seam and the giant thumbnail peels the whole blue back diagonally — pressure differential as her lift. Extended: the atmosphere is laminate — blue peels to sunset, sunset to candy night, night to mango otherspace — she rises through torn corners curling like page turns

The bite does not touch the car at first: everything OUTSIDE peels itself — bark in ribbons, a house shrugging its siding, a hydrant unzipping — the street undressing at once, and the car last, almost shyly

THE BUOYANCY LAW: whatever gets peeled becomes buoyant. Rinds and stickers drift skyward like reverse autumn; kids snatch ascending gummy ribbons out of the air and eat them; the sky islands are where peeled things go. Bens own p2 frame already obeys this — his floating panels, named as physics

The molded seam from the actual gummy appears as a raised ridge on the roof; thumbnail catches it and the material STRETCHES — taut, thinning, glowing amber at the stress point, wobble on release (replaces all tear/perforation physics)

Match cut from her bite to a building down the block with the same bite out of it, candy strata in cross-section; second bite, second building. Variant: her thumbnail splits the candy seam and the street asphalt splits along the same seam in the same frame
The route line peels off the driver's phone screen, exits into real space as a curling candy road, and the car follows its own icon up

Every parked car sealed in a transparent bubble on a foil-backed strip; hers pops through from beneath with the thumb-push motion. NOTE: foil-backed conflicts with no-paper-physics vocabulary — needs gummy-correction or stays a drawer idea

THE PEEL-STYLE LAW: how you peel writes what you become — slow spiral gets deliberate segmented armor; total unwrap gets the full avatar at once; bite-through gets a half-armored hybrid; whole-eat transforms the WORLD instead. Four kids on the bleachers, four styles, four different outcomes
seeds.stimulus: Capri Sun Disrespectoids law, surface banned

seeds.text: Spiral-peeler Raul: armor assembles in a rising helix stripe around his body, and the finished chest-core sweeps the stadium with a slow rotating mango beam — whoever the light touches stands up without knowing why

The whole-biter counterpart: total transformation but TIMED — radiant anime self for exactly as long as the flavor lasts, a dissolving gummy hourglass visible in the chest; when it empties she is ordinary again and immediately reaches for the bag

Transformation as watchmaking: extreme macro of unseen tweezers setting each mango plate like a jewel bearing, escapement clicks syncing to the marching band; wide shot — Raul now moves with second-hand smoothness, a precision instrument

Permuted: Raul peels and the STADIUM transforms — the field floods gently into a mango-colored shallow sea, players wading; Raul stands unchanged at the center, the only dry thing, suddenly the calm eye of it all

TRANSITION DEVICE: the hydro-dip — a vertical film floats across the campus path like a soap membrane; the couple walks through and comes out WEARING the next world (cel, clay, painted); the membrane never breaks, it transfers allegiance. One dip per world
WORLD: the sketch — Johns peel curls into a pencil-line spiral and a drawn hand reaches out of it, pulling them both into rotoscope-line world where only their Peelerz bags stay full color
seeds.stimulus: c111 Take On Me x peel

seeds.text: STRUCTURE: each orbit of the circular dolly crosses a twilight seam and the campus is one era or medium different on the far side; the couple stays mid-stride, one step closer per seam

WORLD: the assembly room — in white void, gentle unseen arms plate the couple with translucent candy-shell panels as they lean in; the kiss lands as the last panels click. TENSION: robot-factory register may read cold for candy
seeds.stimulus: c122 All Is Full of Love

seeds.text: DEVICE: the shared skin — John offers Liz his peeled strip like a borrowed hoodie; worn on her shoulders it renders HER in his medium (cel-shaded girl in a live-action quad); she peels hers for him and he goes painterly. They walk on wearing each others worlds
seeds.stimulus: c205 Hagoromo x c313 borrowed hoodie

seeds.text: The fit test, product-native: John carries a peeled gummy core; Liz has been carrying a peel that fits nothing. On the campus steps her peel wraps his core perfectly — the shoe test restaged in the products two textures. The crowd of mismatched peels behind her tells the story

The flavor-duration avatar named as folk law: transformation lasts while the taste lasts, the way Cinderellas lasts till midnight — and everyone in this world KNOWS the rule, so the crowd counts down his last seconds of mango armor together, delighted
seeds.stimulus: Cox 1893 timed transformation (midnight rule) - ancestry for s073

seeds.text: Peelers vs Biters as school tribes — friendly rivalry: the peeler side of the cafeteria grows terraced and layered, the biter side pops instant and total; the treaty is a couple sharing one candy — she peels it, he bites it

The cloak: a peel worn hood-up renders the wearer in the fruits world-grammar — cloaked in mango, the hallway ripens as he passes; shed the cloak and the world snaps ordinary. Catskin and Hagoromo ancestry: the garment IS the boundary

WORLD: the Garden of Gummy Delights — a Bosch panorama in candy materiality: teens picnicking inside a hollowed lychee the size of a gazebo, riding translucent gummy birds, climbing in and out of giant berries, candy-glass tubes linking fruit dwellings; dense, benign, jewel-lit

Fruits peel THEMSELVES: outside the window a fruit stand molts — an orange spirals open unassisted and steps off brighter; a pear sheds its skin like a coat checked at a door. The produce demonstrates the move; nobody taught it

SPINE: the same peel gesture at four scales in one spot — thumbnail on candy, hand on car roof, crane-arm on a building, sky-wide on the cloud layer — the whole commercial is one gesture repeated bigger (kin: Cog relay, continuous-tether)

The candy as the magical item of a teen fairy tale: an object with RULES everyone somehow knows — carried like a talisman, traded like a favor, opened only at the right moment; a fruit compressed into a pocketable marvel, the nut that contains the dress. The world treats one gummy with the gravity of a ring in a fairy tale
seeds.stimulus: David riff: magical item (+ Cox c613 nut-releases-dress)

continuous-material-action.evidence: T01 continuous peel; still-peel-spinning-world; climbable-pour; monitored-peel-event; pre-laid-ribbon — five distinct executions of one invariant
